intro: Use the conditional with the imperfect subjunctive for hypothetical situations: si tuviera tiempo, viajar?a. The si clause gives the condition; the conditional gives the result.

overview.body: Use the conditional with the imperfect subjunctive for hypothetical situations: si tuviera tiempo, viajar?a. The si clause gives the condition; the conditional gives the result. Focus on the message first, then choose the form that fits the context.

uses.heading: Real use
uses.body: Use this pattern when conditional plus imperfect subjunctive helps express a real message, not just a grammar label.

uses.heading: Common context
uses.body: This form often appears in everyday explanations, plans, advice, and reactions depending on the sentence.
